title: La Naga dans la culture khme: serpent sacré de pouvoir, de protection
highlights:
“Naga” originates from the Sanskrit word for serpent. In Khmer belief—blending elements from Hinduism, Buddhism, and indigenous animism—the Naga is a semi-divine serpent being, usually portrayed with multiple heads, ranging from three to as many as nine or more. In Hindu cosmology, Nagas are underworld deities who dwell in water and guard treasures. In Buddhist tradition, the Naga is a protector of the Dharma, sometimes even transforming into a human form to assist  ...
